title: Customizing a process WBS per project
overview: Explains how a reusable process WBS is tailored to each project's scope.
detailed_summary: A process WBS is ideally a blueprint that includes every type of deliverable that could be part of performing the process. For each project where the process is used, the project's scope determines which process deliverables are in scope and which are out of scope. This reduces the likelihood of overlooking a process deliverable and encourages consistent execution across projects.
content: A reusable process WBS should be broad enough to cover all deliverable types relevant to the methodology. Individual projects then customize that blueprint by keeping only the deliverables that apply to their scope. This approach supports consistency while allowing flexibility. It also reduces the risk that a project team will inadvertently miss a process deliverable because the standard blueprint already makes it visible.
relationships: Project-level tailoring aligns process deliverables to actual scope
search_queries: How do you tailor a process WBS?
Why use a reusable WBS blueprint?
How does a process WBS reduce omissions?
common_questions: How is a standard process WBS used on individual projects?
Why not create a new WBS from scratch each time?
can_answer: How reusable process WBS templates work
Why tailoring is needed per project
How process WBS reduces overlooked deliverables
examples: A workplace relocation blueprint may contain all possible relocation deliverables, while a specific relocation project includes only those actually needed.
best_practices: Maintain a broad process WBS blueprint.
Tailor it per project by removing out-of-scope deliverables.
warnings: Using a generic process WBS without tailoring can include unnecessary scope.
limitations: A reusable blueprint still requires project-specific review and decomposition.
troubleshooting: If teams are overlooking process steps, verify whether they are using and tailoring the standard process WBS appropriately.
decision_rules: Treat the reusable process WBS as a starting point, not an automatic final scope for every project.
